questions.q: The Pandavas came to live in Ekachakra after escaping which attempt on their lives?
options: The poisoning of Bhima at Pramanakoti
The burning of the house of lac at Varanavata
An ambush on the road to Hastinapura
A night attack on their forest camp
questions.explanation: They fled the lac palace through a tunnel and wandered in disguise until they settled in Ekachakra, with the world believing them dead.

questions.q: In what disguise did the Pandavas and Kunti live among the people of Ekachakra?
options: As traveling merchants
As cowherds serving the town
As brahmin mendicants
As hired soldiers of the king
questions.explanation: The five brothers and their mother passed as a poor brahmin family, which let them live unnoticed on alms.

questions.q: How did the Pandavas support themselves during their stay in Ekachakra?
options: By serving in the local court
By farming a borrowed field
By selling pots in the market
By begging alms through the town
questions.explanation: Each day they went out begging like brahmins and brought everything they received back to Kunti at evening.

questions.q: Who divided the alms each night in Ekachakra, setting aside half for Bhima alone?
options: Kunti
Yudhishthira
Draupadi
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: Kunti split the day's collection, giving half to the ever-hungry Bhima and sharing the rest among herself and the other four brothers.

questions.q: Which demon forced Ekachakra to pay a regular tribute that included a human life?
options: Hidimba
Kirmira
Bakasura
Jatasura
questions.explanation: The rakshasa Baka, or Bakasura, demanded food and a human victim from the town in exchange for leaving it otherwise in peace.

questions.q: Which sage guided the Pandavas to Ekachakra during their wanderings?
options: Narada
Vyasa
Durvasa
Vasishtha
questions.explanation: Vyasa met the fugitive family, directed them to Ekachakra, and continued to watch over their steps.

questions.q: Why was Ekachakra a safe hiding place for the Pandavas at that point in the story?
options: The world believed they had died in the lac house fire
Duryodhana had sworn them safe passage
The town was ruled by Kunti's kinsmen
Bhishma had secretly arranged their shelter
questions.explanation: The bodies of a nishada woman and her five sons found in the ashes were taken for Kunti and the Pandavas, so no one was hunting them.

questions.q: In whose home did the Pandavas lodge during their stay in Ekachakra?
options: A fisherman who worked the river
A brahmin who took them in as guests
A blacksmith near the town gate
A wealthy grain merchant
questions.explanation: In Vyasa's epic they stay in the house of a brahmin family, though some later retellings place them in a potter's house instead.

questions.q: Which Pandava did Kunti send to face the demon who taxed Ekachakra in human lives?
options: Arjuna
Yudhishthira
Nakula
Bhima
questions.explanation: Kunti chose Bhima, confident that his enormous strength could end the rakshasa's tyranny over the town.

questions.q: Where did the Pandavas go after their stay in Ekachakra ended?
options: To Panchala for Draupadi's swayamvara
To Hastinapura to reclaim their throne
To Dwarka to seek Krishna's help
To Virata to serve in disguise
questions.explanation: News of Draupadi's swayamvara drew them to Kampilya in Panchala, still wearing their brahmin disguise.

questions.q: How did Kunti first learn that the family sheltering the Pandavas was in danger?
options: A royal herald announced the family's turn in the street
Bhima saw the tribute cart being loaded
She overheard the household weeping together inside the house
Vyasa appeared and warned her in a dream
questions.explanation: Kunti heard the host family lamenting aloud and gently pressed the brahmin until he told her of the demon's tribute and their turn to die.

questions.q: What tribute did Ekachakra send regularly to the rakshasa Baka?
options: A chest of gold and ten goats
A cartload of food, two buffaloes, and the person who delivered it
A hundred jars of ghee and a milk cow
A season's harvest of rice and a young bull
questions.explanation: The demon ate the food, the buffaloes, and the unlucky human who drove the cart to him.

questions.q: Why had the people of Ekachakra agreed to feed the rakshasa at such a terrible price?
options: Baka shielded the town from enemies and other demons in return for the tribute
They hoped to slowly poison him through the food
Their king had decreed the tribute as a royal tax
A sage had cursed the town to serve the demon
questions.explanation: With their own king too feeble to protect them, the citizens bought safety by feeding Baka, who kept invaders and other rakshasas away.

questions.q: Which member of the grieving host family waved a blade of grass and vowed to kill the demon, making the others smile through tears?
options: The brahmin himself
His wife
The young daughter
The infant son
questions.explanation: While the father, mother, and daughter each insisted on dying for the others, the toddler's brave babble briefly lightened their grief.

questions.q: How did Kunti justify offering Bhima for the tribute when their host's turn came?
options: They owed the host a debt of gratitude, and she had full faith in Bhima's strength
A prophecy said Bhima could never die in Ekachakra
Bhima had sworn an oath to slay every rakshasa he met
The town had promised the family a reward of gold
questions.explanation: Kunti argued that repaying their host's kindness was dharma and that Bhima's proven might made the risk small.

questions.q: Which brother objected when he learned Kunti had pledged Bhima to deliver the tribute?
options: Arjuna
Yudhishthira
Nakula
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira called the decision rash until Kunti explained her confidence in Bhima and their duty to the family sheltering them.

questions.q: Who first told the Pandavas in Ekachakra about Drupada's fire-born children and the coming swayamvara?
options: A messenger sent by King Drupada
A spy dispatched by Vidura
A wandering brahmin who visited their dwelling
A merchant caravan from Kampilya
questions.explanation: A visiting brahmin's tales of Panchala stirred the household, and Vyasa soon arrived to advise the journey to Draupadi's swayamvara.

questions.q: How did the people of Ekachakra explain the demon's death, since the Pandavas' role was kept secret?
options: They believed the gods had struck the demon down
They assumed the king's soldiers had ambushed him
They thought rival demons had killed him in a feud
They credited a mantra-knowing brahmin who had volunteered to deliver the tribute
questions.explanation: The host kept Kunti's secret and told the townsfolk that a high-souled brahmin with mantra powers had taken the food and slain the rakshasa.

questions.q: Where did the people of Ekachakra discover the demon's enormous corpse?
options: Floating in the river outside town
At the gate of the town
In the marketplace square
At the door of the king's residence
questions.explanation: Bhima dragged the body to the town gate and left it there, and the astonished citizens found it the next morning.

questions.q: How often did a given household's turn to supply the demon's tribute come around?
options: Every household served once each month
Each family served twice a year
A family's turn came only after many years
The richest families served every week
questions.explanation: The tribute rotated house by house through the town, so a family's turn came rarely, but when it came one member had to go and die.

questions.q: Which earlier feat of Bhima did Kunti recall when defending her decision to send him against the demon?
options: He had slain the rakshasa Hidimba with his bare hands
He had won the archery prize at the princes' tournament
He had tamed a rogue war elephant in Hastinapura
He had bested Drona in a practice duel
questions.explanation: Kunti reminded Yudhishthira that Bhima had already killed the mighty Hidimba in the forest and had carried the whole family to safety.

questions.q: Which episode took place between the escape from the house of lac and the arrival at Ekachakra?
options: The Yaksha's riddles at the poisoned lake
Arjuna's penance for Shiva's weapon
The burning of the Khandava forest
Bhima's marriage to the rakshasi Hidimbi
questions.explanation: In the forest on their flight, Bhima killed Hidimba, married Hidimbi, and fathered Ghatotkacha before the family moved on to Ekachakra.

questions.q: After the demon of Ekachakra was slain, what did Bhima demand from the dead rakshasa's kin?
options: That they give up preying on humans and leave the town in peace
That they serve the Pandavas in the coming war
That they pay the town a yearly tribute of gold
That they withdraw to the forests of the far south
questions.explanation: Bhima made Baka's terrified followers promise to stop killing humans, freeing Ekachakra from rakshasa fear for good.

questions.q: Where did the rakshasa who terrorized Ekachakra make his dwelling?
options: In the ruined palace of an old king
Beneath the ford of the river
In the forest outside the town
On a cliff overlooking the gate
questions.explanation: Baka lived in the wild country outside Ekachakra, and the tribute cart had to be driven out to his forest haunt.

questions.q: What did Vyasa do for the Pandavas when he brought them to Ekachakra?
options: He gave them an inexhaustible vessel of food
He settled them in their host's home and promised to return with guidance
He taught them the local dialect and customs
He gave them new names to use in the town
questions.explanation: Vyasa personally arranged their lodging, comforted Kunti, and told them to wait there until he came again with further counsel.

questions.q: With which modern region is the town of Ekachakra most often identified in later tradition?
options: The Kashmir valley
Coastal Konkan in Maharashtra
The desert country of Rajasthan
The Birbhum district of West Bengal
questions.explanation: Tradition most often points to Ekachakra in Birbhum, West Bengal, though some accounts place the site near Etawah in Uttar Pradesh instead.

questions.q: Where were the other four Pandavas when Kunti resolved to send Bhima against the demon?
options: Out begging alms in the town
Hunting game in the forest
Attending the king's court
Standing guard at the house
questions.explanation: Only Bhima was at home with Kunti that day, and Yudhishthira learned of her pledge when the brothers returned at evening.

questions.q: What was Yudhishthira's first reaction on hearing that Bhima would be sent to the demon?
options: He praised the plan as worthy of their line
He rebuked Kunti for gambling with the brother who was their strength
He insisted on going in Bhima's place
He proposed the family flee the town that night
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira protested that all their hopes rested on Bhima's strength, until Kunti's reasoning about gratitude and merit won him over.

questions.q: Within which sub-book of the Adi Parva is the Ekachakra episode and the demon's death narrated?
options: Sambhava Parva
Jatugriha Parva
Baka-vadha Parva
Viduragamana Parva
questions.explanation: The Baka-vadha Parva, the section on the slaying of Baka, comes after the Jatugriha Parva of the lac house and the Hidimba-vadha Parva of the forest episode.

questions.q: How did Kunti persuade the brahmin host to let her son take his family's place on the tribute cart?
options: She revealed that her sons were the lost Pandava princes
She offered the family a purse of gold for the risk
She said her son was fated to die young in any case
She claimed her son had mantra-won powers that could defeat the rakshasa
questions.explanation: Kunti concealed their true identity, crediting secret mantras for her son's powers, and asked the family to tell no one who had done the deed.
